campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the city's business license tax rates for the upcoming fiscal year. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to the tax code, and council members debated and voted on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Fiscal year budget adjustments
Municipal code amendments
Finance department presentation
Emergency preparedness coordination

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the usual first reading step and planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on new ordinance
Council waived first reading to speed up approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting minutes and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized several construction contracts and professional service agreements to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Approved town improvement projects and planning
Approved three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve various city projects and agreements. Key items included moving forward with replacing an elevator at the Terminal, reviewing plans for cleaning up homeless encampments, updating a digital billboard agreement with Clear Channel, and approving a memorial event for fallen firefighters.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - moving forward with repairs/upgrades
Encampment Cleanup Services - getting proposals for removing homeless camps
Digital Signage Agreement - updating contract for large electronic billboards on city property
Light the Night Event - approving a firefighter memorial event and accepting donations for it

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes, hear directly from residents about their concerns, and review funding requests from neighborhood groups for the upcoming 2026-27 budget year. The council discussed which neighborhood projects and programs should receive city funding and reviewed attendance at recent neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 budget
Public comment period for residents to speak directly to council
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Neighborhood meeting attendance and participation
Staff reports and committee updates on ongoing city business

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to watch and participate either in person or online before the meeting concluded.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss buying or selling property at 10480 Finch Avenue with the local school district, with the city manager and attorney leading negotiations over price and payment terms. The council also reviewed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and discussed plans to get more grant funding for it.
keyTopics: Property deal negotiations with Cupertino Union School District for 10480 Finch Avenue
Determining purchase price and payment terms for the Finch Avenue property
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup games. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract finalized with Santa Clara firefighters
One agenda item was withdrawn by city staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas city leaders met to plan the city's spending for the next several years. They reviewed a 5-year plan for big projects like roads and buildings, discussed the budget for the next fiscal year, approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, and made updates to how the City Council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: 5-year Capital Improvement Plan (2027-2031) - major projects and infrastructure spending
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget presentation and approval
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adoption
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and rules

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss routine business items and review a major development proposal for 4103 Old Trace Road. The council considered a plan to subdivide a vacant lot into nine single-family homes and seven smaller accessory units, along with a new private street to serve the development.
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre lot into 9 residential lots at 4103 Old Trace Road
Proposal to build 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Creation of private street to access the new development
Leadership elections for City Council chair and vice chair positions
